name: grip

metadata.name: grip
metadata.summary: Render local readme files before sending off to GitHub.

documentation:
Grip
----

Render local readme files before sending off to GitHub.


Grip is easy to set up
``````````````````````

::

    $ pip install grip
    $ cd myproject
    $ grip


Links
`````

* `Website <http://github.com/joeyespo/grip>`_

Renders the specified markdown content and embedded styles.

Raises TypeError if text is not a Unicode string.

Get style URLs from the source HTML page and specified cached asset

variables.name: grip.__version__
documentation: noqa

functions.name: grip.browser.is_server_running
documentation:
Checks whether a server is currently listening on the specified
host and port.

functions.name: grip.browser.wait_for_server
documentation:
Blocks until a local server is listening on the specified
host and port. Set cancel_event to cancel the wait.

This is intended to be used in conjunction with running
the Flask server.

functions.name: grip.browser.start_browser
documentation: Opens the specified URL in a new browser window.

functions.name: grip.browser.wait_and_start_browser
documentation:
Waits for the server to run and then opens the specified address in
the browser. Set cancel_event to cancel the wait.

functions.name: grip.browser.start_browser_when_ready
documentation:
Starts a thread that waits for the server then opens the specified
address in the browser. Set cancel_event to cancel the wait. The
started thread object is returned.

functions.name: grip.api.create_app
documentation: Creates a Grip application with the specified overrides.

functions.name: grip.api.serve
documentation:
Starts a server to render the specified file or directory containing
a README.

functions.name: grip.api.clear_cache
documentation: Clears the cached styles and assets.

functions.name: grip.api.render_page
documentation: Renders the specified markup text to an HTML page and returns it.

functions.name: grip.api.render_content
documentation: Renders the specified markup and returns the result.

functions.name: grip.api.export
documentation: Exports the rendered HTML to a file.

modules.name: grip.settings
documentation:
Default Configuration

Do NOT change the values here for risk of accidentally committing them.
Override them using command-line arguments or with a settings_local.py in
this directory or in ~/.grip/settings.py instead.

modules.name: grip._compat
documentation: TODO: Use Werkzeug's directly after dropping support for older Flask versions

functions.name: grip._compat.safe_join
documentation:
Safely join zero or more untrusted path components to a base
directory to avoid escaping the base directory.

```
param directory

The trusted base directory.

param pathnames

The untrusted path components relative to the
base directory.

return

A safe path.
```
Use port of Flask 2.0 safe_join to match behavior

classes.name: grip.app.Grip

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._redirect_to_subpath
documentation:
Redirects to the specified subpath, which is the relative path
part of the root location (i.e. the current working directory
or the path part of a URL excluding the initial '/').

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._render_asset
documentation: Renders the specified cache file.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._render_rate_limit_page
documentation: Renders the rate limit page.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._get_styles
documentation:
Gets the content of the given list of style URLs and
inlines assets.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._inline_styles
documentation:
Downloads the assets from the style URL list, clears it, and adds
each style with its embedded asset to the literal style list.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip._retrieve_styles
documentation:
Retrieves the style URLs from the source and caches them. This
is called before the first request is dispatched.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip.default_renderer
documentation:
Returns the default renderer using the current config.

This is only used if renderer is set to None in the constructor.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip.default_asset_manager
documentation:
Returns the default asset manager using the current config.

This is only used if asset_manager is set to None in the constructor.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip.add_content_types
documentation:
Adds the application/x-font-woff and application/octet-stream
content types if they are missing.

Override to add additional content types on initialization.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip.render
documentation:
Renders the application and returns the HTML unicode that would
normally appear when visiting in the browser.

methods.name: grip.app.Grip.run
documentation: Starts a server to render the README.

A Flask application that can serve the specified file or directory
containing a README.

classes.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.normalize_subpath
documentation:
Returns the normalized subpath.

This allows Readme files to be inferred from directories while
still allowing relative paths to work properly.

Override to change the default behavior of returning the
specified subpath as-is.

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.filename_for
documentation:
Returns the relative filename for the specified subpath, or None
if the file does not exist.

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.mimetype_for
documentation: Gets the mimetype for the specified subpath.

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.is_binary
documentation: Gets whether the specified subpath is a supported binary file.

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.last_updated
documentation:
Returns the time of the last modification of the Readme or
specified subpath. None is returned if the reader doesn't
support modification tracking.

The format of return value is dependent on the implementing
reader. It can be any object as long as equality indicates
that the content was not updated.

methods.name: grip.readers.ReadmeReader.read
documentation:
Returns the UTF-8 content of the specified subpath, or None if
subpath does not exist.
Reads Readme content from a URL subpath.

classes.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader._find_file
documentation:
Gets the full path and extension, or None if a README file could not
be found at the specified path.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader._resolve_readme
documentation:
Returns the path if it's a file; otherwise, looks for a compatible
README file in the directory specified by path.

If path is None, the current working directory is used.

If silent is set, the default relative filename will be returned
if path is a directory or None if it does not exist.

Raises ReadmeNotFoundError if no compatible README file can be
found and silent is False.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader._read_text
documentation:
Helper that reads the UTF-8 content of the specified file, or
None if the file doesn't exist. This returns a unicode string.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader._read_binary
documentation:
Helper that reads the binary content of the specified file, or
None if the file doesn't exist. This returns a byte string.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.normalize_subpath
documentation:
Normalizes the specified subpath, or None if subpath is None.

This allows Readme files to be inferred from directories while
still allowing relative paths to work properly.

Raises werkzeug.exceptions.NotFound if the resulting path
would fall out of the root directory.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.readme_for
documentation:
Returns the full path for the README file for the specified
subpath, or the root filename if subpath is None.

Raises ReadmeNotFoundError if a README for the specified subpath
does not exist.

Raises werkzeug.exceptions.NotFound if the resulting path
would fall out of the root directory.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.filename_for
documentation:
Returns the relative filename for the specified subpath, or the
root filename if subpath is None.

Raises werkzeug.exceptions.NotFound if the resulting path
would fall out of the root directory.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.is_binary
documentation: Gets whether the specified subpath is a supported binary file.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.last_updated
documentation:
Returns the time of the last modification of the Readme or
specified subpath, or None if the file does not exist.

The return value is a number giving the number of seconds since
the epoch (see the time module).

Raises werkzeug.exceptions.NotFound if the resulting path
would fall out of the root directory.

methods.name: grip.readers.DirectoryReader.read
documentation:
Returns the UTF-8 content of the specified subpath.

subpath is expected to already have been normalized.

Raises ReadmeNotFoundError if a README for the specified subpath
does not exist.

Raises werkzeug.exceptions.NotFound if the resulting path
would fall out of the root directory.
Reads Readme files from URL subpaths.

classes.name: grip.readers.TextReader

methods.name: grip.readers.TextReader.filename_for
documentation:
Returns the display filename, or None if subpath is specified
since subpaths are not supported for text readers.

methods.name: grip.readers.TextReader.read
documentation:
Returns the UTF-8 Readme content.

Raises ReadmeNotFoundError if subpath is specified since
subpaths are not supported for text readers.
Reads Readme content from the provided unicode string.

classes.name: grip.readers.StdinReader

methods.name: grip.readers.StdinReader.read
documentation:
Returns the UTF-8 Readme content.

Raises ReadmeNotFoundError if subpath is specified since
subpaths are not supported for text readers.

methods.name: grip.readers.StdinReader.read_stdin
documentation: Reads STDIN until the end of input and returns a unicode string.
Reads Readme text from STDIN.

Processes the HTML rendered by the GitHub API, patching
any inconsistencies from the main site.

modules.name: grip.command
documentation:
grip.command
~~~~~~~~~~~~

Implements the command-line interface for Grip.


Usage:
  grip [options] [<path>] [<address>]
  grip -V | --version
  grip -h | --help

Where:
  <path> is a file to render or a directory containing README.md (- for stdin)
  <address> is what to listen on, of the form <host>[:<port>], or just <port>

Options:
  --user-content    Render as user-content like comments or issues.
  --context=<repo>  The repository context, only taken into account
                    when using --user-content.
  --user=<username> A GitHub username for API authentication. If used
                    without the --pass option, an upcoming password
                    input will be necessary.
  --pass=<password> A GitHub password or auth token for API auth.
  --wide            Renders wide, i.e. when the side nav is collapsed.
                    This only takes effect when --user-content is used.
  --clear           Clears the cached styles and assets and exits.
  --export          Exports to <path>.html or README.md instead of
                    serving, optionally using [<address>] as the out
                    file (- for stdout).
  --no-inline       Link to styles instead inlining when using --export.
  -b --browser      Open a tab in the browser after the server starts.
  --api-url=<url>   Specify a different base URL for the github API,
                    for example that of a Github Enterprise instance.
                    Default is the public API: https://api.github.com
  --title=<title>   Manually sets the page's title.
                    The default is the filename.
  --norefresh       Do not automatically refresh the Readme content when
                    the file changes.
  --quiet           Do not print to the terminal.

functions.name: grip.command.main
documentation: The entry point of the application.

modules.name: grip.vendor.mdx_urlize
documentation:
A more liberal autolinker Inspired by Django's urlize function. Positive examples: ```pycon >>> import markdown >>> md = markdown.Markdown(extensions=['urlize']) ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('http://example.com/') u'

http://example.com/

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('go to http://example.com') u'

go to http://example.com

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('example.com') u'

example.com

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('example.net') u'

example.net

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('www.example.us') u'

www.example.us

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('(www.example.us/path/?name=val)') u'

(www.example.us/path/?name=val)

' ``` ```pycon >>> md.convert('go to now!') u'

go to http://example.com now!

' ``` Negative examples: ```pycon >>> md.convert('del.icio.us') u'

del.icio.us

' ```

classes.name: grip.vendor.mdx_urlize.UrlizePattern
documentation: Return a link Element given an autolink (`http://example/com`).

classes.name: grip.vendor.mdx_urlize.UrlizeExtension

methods.name: grip.vendor.mdx_urlize.UrlizeExtension.extendMarkdown
documentation: Replace autolink with UrlizePattern
Urlize Extension for Python-Markdown.
